title: Problem 1.1 (Trotignon)
statement_text: Is $\chi(G)$ bounded for every triangle-free sun-free graph $G$?
context_text: Trotignon posed this problem at the 2025 Barbados Graph Theory Workshop. A $t$-sun ($t\geq 4$) is the graph obtained from a $t$-vertex cycle by adding a degree-one neighbor for each vertex; a graph is sun-free if it contains no induced $t$-sun for any $t\geq 4$.
paper_contribution: Proves that every triangle-free graph of chromatic number at least $48$ has an induced subgraph that is either a $t$-sun for some $t\geq 5$ or a $4$-sunspot (a $4$-sun with a single degree-one vertex deleted); more generally, for all $\ell\geq 5$ there exists $c(\ell)\in\mathbb{N}$ such that every triangle-free graph with $\chi\geq c(\ell)$ has such an induced subgraph.
paper_title: Suns in triangle-free graphs of large chromatic number

title: Problem 1.4 (Trotignon)
statement_text: Is the class of all net-free sun-free graphs $\chi$-bounded?
context_text: Trotignon proposed this as an extension of Problem 1.1 to graphs of bounded clique number. A net is the graph obtained from a $3$-vertex cycle by adding a degree-one neighbor for each vertex of the cycle; a graph class $\mathcal{C}$ is $\chi$-bounded if there is a function $f:\mathbb{N}\rightarrow\mathbb{N}$ such that $\chi(G)\leq f(\omega(G))$ for every $G\in\mathcal{C}$.
paper_contribution: Derives a partial answer analogous to their main Theorem 1.3 by combining it with Hajebi's result on bull-free graphs (Theorem 1.5), extending the chromatic-boundedness conclusion to the net-free sun-free setting under analogous exclusion conditions.
paper_title: Suns in triangle-free graphs of large chromatic number

title: Informal conjecture on sun-free $\chi$-boundedness without net exclusion
statement_text: It could be true that the class of all sun-free graphs is already $\chi$-bounded.
context_text: After stating Problem 1.4, the authors remark that they are not sure why the net needs to be excluded, suggesting that sun-freeness alone might suffice for $\chi$-boundedness without additionally requiring net-freeness.
notes: Expressed as a tentative remark ("it could be true that") following Problem 1.4, not as a labelled conjecture environment.
paper_title: Suns in triangle-free graphs of large chromatic number
